name: Maple Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in a medium saucepan.
2. Cook and stir over medium-high heat until boiling.
3. Attach candy thermometer to the side of the pan.
4. Cook and stir over medium heat, until the maple syrup reaches 238°F (155°C) on the candy thermometer (soft ball stage), which takes about 25 to 35 minutes.
5. Remove from heat and cool without stirring until it reaches a lukewarm temperature of 110°F (45°C), taking approximately 50 to 60 minutes.
6. Remove the candy thermometer and beat the mixture with a wooden spoon until the color lightens and the fudge begins to set quickly.
7. Press the mixture into a well-greased 8-inch square pan, making sure it is in squares before the fudge sets.
8. Allow to firm up completely, then cut into squares.
This recipe makes 20 to 25 pieces.
